title: Macro Minute: What Does The Q4 QRA Signal For US Liquidity?

42 macro so today's key macro questions
are what does part one of the qra signal
about the outlook for us liquidity
spending on AI still robust and is the
US Labor Market still resilient our
answers are part one of the Q4 qra
suggests the US Treasury will attempt to
incrementally crowd out the global
private sector and drain us liquidity in
q1 Via a $277 billion increase in net
marketable borrowing and $150 billion
increase in the TGA this compares to a
revised $216 billion reduction in net
marketable borrowing and a planned $186
billion reduction in the TGA in Q4 we do
not believe the treasury will be
successful in its attempt to
incrementally crowd out the global
private sector and drain us liquidity in
q1 due to what is likely to be another
lengthy debt selling negotiation in the
Senate investors should expect roughly
700 billion dollar of TGA spin down in
the first few months of 2025 which will
be a boost to us liquidity alphabet uh
Google reports earnings today kicking
off a week that features results from
five Magnificent Seven names and
companies representing a combined 42% of
S&P 500 market cap robust productivity
dynamic has continued to underpin our
bullish bias on the Us corporate profit
cycle there's been a cons there has been
considerable slack created in the labor
market but that slack has come from a
reduction in job openings and turnover
not outright firings this is the first
time in the history of these time series
where M fire rings have materially
diverged from job openings and turnover
we have hypothesized that labor hoarding
will persist until the corporate profit
cycle breaks down it is currently in an
accelerating
upturn transitioning to a 42 macro
dashboard here as always we'll wrap up
with a question from our community uh
